title: What's Your Toxic Developer Trait?
description: Every dev has one. Find yours and share it with your team (sorry not sorry)
viralHook: I got 'Premature Optimizer' — I'm in this quiz and I don't like it 💀

questions.question: Someone submits a PR with 3 files changed. You:
options: Approve it — looks good
Leave 47 comments about naming conventions
Rewrite the whole thing in the review
Ask why they didn't use the pattern from that blog post you read
questions.explanation: Ship it. Perfect is the enemy of good.

questions.question: The build is broken on main. You:
options: Fix it immediately
Check git blame first to see who broke it
Post in Slack: 'who broke the build?'
Secretly deploy your fix and say nothing
questions.explanation: Fix first, blame never. The build doesn't care about your ego.

questions.question: A non-technical stakeholder asks how long a feature will take:
options: Give an honest estimate with caveats
'It depends' (classic)
Triple your estimate, deliver early, look like a hero
Show them the Jira board and watch their eyes glaze over
questions.explanation: Honest estimates build trust. Sandbagging builds resentment.

questions.question: You discover legacy code with no tests:
options: Add tests before touching anything
Rewrite it from scratch (it's 'tech debt')
Add a TODO comment and move on
This is my code from 6 months ago
questions.explanation: Test first, then refactor. Rewrites are almost always a bad idea.

questions.question: Your preferred way to handle disagreements in code review:
options: Discuss the tradeoffs professionally
'Per the style guide, section 4.2.1...'
Passive-aggressive 'nit:' comments
Just approve it and complain in the team retro
questions.explanation: Technical disagreements should be about tradeoffs, not egos.

questions.question: How do you feel about meetings?
options: Necessary for alignment
Most could be an email
I decline all meetings and respond async
I have meetings about meetings
questions.explanation: Some meetings matter. Most don't. Protect your focus time.
